structured.name: Retrieve Log
structured.objective: To enable the CSMS retrieving of log information from a Charging Station.
structured.description:
This use case covers the functionality of getting log information from a Charging Station. The
CSMS can request a Charging Station to upload a file with log information to a given location
(URL). The format of this log file is not prescribed. The Charging Station uploads a log file and
gives information about the status of the upload by sending status notifications to the CSMS.
scenario: 1. The CSMS sends a GetLogRequest to the Charging Station.
2. The Charging Station responds with a GetLogResponse.
3. The Charging Station sends a LogStatusNotificationRequest with the status Uploading

4. The CSMS responds with a LogStatusNotificationResponse acknowledging the status update
request.
6. The Charging Station sends LogStatusNotificationRequest with the status Uploaded.

7. The CSMS responds with LogStatusNotificationResponse, acknowledging the status update
request.
structured.prerequisites:
- Diagnostics information is available for upload.
- URL to upload file to is reachable and exists.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
Log file successfully uploaded.
Failure postcondition:
Log file not successfully uploaded and failed.
structured.error_handling:
When the upload fails and the transfer protocol supports "resume" the Charging Station is
RECOMMENDED to try to resume before aborting the upload.
structured.remarks:
As an example in this use case the requestId = 123, but this could be any value.
When a Charging Station is requested to upload a log file, the CSMS supplies in the request an
URL where the Charging Station SHALL upload the file. The URL also contains the protocol which
must be used to upload the file.
The FTP URL is of format: ftp://User:password@host:port/path in which the parts User:
password@, :password or :port may be excluded.
The Charging Station has a required Configuration Variable that reports which file transfer
protocols it supports: FileTransferProtocols
The format of the log file is not prescribed.
FTP needs to be able to use Passive FTP, to be able to transverse over as much different
typologies as possible.

requirements.precondition:
Upon receipt of a GetLogRequest AND
if the requested log information is
available
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a
GetLogResponse stating the name of the file and
status Accepted.

The Charging Station SHALL start uploading a
single log file to the specified location

requirements.precondition:
N01.FR.02
AND
The GetLogRequest contained
logType SecurityLog
requirements.definition: The Charging Station SHALL upload its security log

requirements.precondition:
N01.FR.02
AND
The GetLogRequest contained
logType DiagnosticsLog
requirements.definition: The Charging Station SHALL upload its diagnostics.

requirements.precondition:
Upon receipt of a GetLogRequest AND
if the requested log information is
NOT available
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a
GetLogResponse WITH status Rejected.

Every LogStatusNotificationRequest sent for a log
upload SHALL contain the same requestId as the
GetLogRequest that started this log upload.

requirements.precondition:
When uploading a log document is
started
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status
Uploading.

requirements.precondition:
When a log document is uploaded
successfully
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status
Uploaded.

requirements.precondition: When uploading a log document failed
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status
UploadFailure, BadMessage,
PermissionDenied OR
NotSupportedOperation.

requirements.precondition:
When a Charging Station is
assembling or uploading the log file
AND
the Charging Station receives a new
GetLogRequest
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHOULD cancel the ongoing
log file upload AND respond with GetLogResponse
with status AcceptedCanceled.

The field requestId in LogStatusNotificationRequest
is mandatory, unless the message was triggered by
a TriggerMessageRequest AND there is no log
upload ongoing.

It is RECOMMENDED that Charging Station and
CSMS support at least HTTP(s) as transport
mechanism for the log file upload

Charging Station SHALL at least support the CSMS
trust chain for secure transports

It is RECOMMENDED that Charging Station
supports the usual CAs provided by the operating
system

requirements.precondition:
When CSMS requires basic
authorization for the upload
requirements.definition:
CSMS is RECOMMENDED to require a different
basic authorization password for the upload, then
the one used for OCPP connectivity.

Is is RECOMMENDED that CSMS accepts both PUT
and POST requests for uploads from Charging
Station.

requirements.precondition:
When Charging Station uses a
HTTP(s) POST request to upload the
log file
requirements.definition:
Charging Station SHALL provide at least the
following attributes: Content-Type: (e.g.
application/octet-stream) and Content-
Disposition: with a specification of the
filename.

requirements.precondition:
N01.FR.12 AND
Charging Station cancels the log file
upload
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status =
AcceptedCanceled.

requirements.precondition:
N01.FR.18 AND
the GetLogRequest
log.remoteLocation string ends with a
slash character ("/")
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL append the filename
that it returned in the GetLogResponse filename
field to this string.

requirements.precondition:
N01.FR.18 AND
the file to upload exceeds the
threshold of 1MB (1024 * 1024 bytes)
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY include the "Expect: 100-
continue" header in the HTTP PUT request.

The Charging Station MAY implement a mechanism
to resume HTTP uploads, if it can identify whether
the upload server supports the same mechanism.

chunks.content:
# N01 - Retrieve Log

**Objective:** To enable the CSMS retrieving of log information from a Charging Station.

**Description:** This use case covers the functionality of getting log information from a Charging Station. The
CSMS can request a Charging Station to upload a file with log information to a given location
(URL). The format of this log file is not prescribed. The Charging Station uploads a log file and
gives information about the status of the upload by sending status notifications to the CSMS.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The CSMS sends a GetLogRequest to the Charging Station.
2. The Charging Station responds with a GetLogResponse.
3. The Charging Station sends a LogStatusNotificationRequest with the status Uploading
4. The CSMS responds with a LogStatusNotificationResponse acknowledging the status update
request.
5. Uploading of the diagnostics files.
6. The Charging Station sends LogStatusNotificationRequest with the status Uploaded.
7. The CSMS responds with LogStatusNotificationResponse, acknowledging the status update
request.

**Prerequisites:** - Diagnostics information is available for upload.
- URL to upload file to is reachable and exists.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
Log file successfully uploaded.
Failure postcondition:
Log file not successfully uploaded and failed.

**Error handling:** When the upload fails and the transfer protocol supports "resume" the Charging Station is
RECOMMENDED to try to resume before aborting the upload.

**Remarks:** As an example in this use case the requestId = 123, but this could be any value.
When a Charging Station is requested to upload a log file, the CSMS supplies in the request an
URL where the Charging Station SHALL upload the file. The URL also contains the protocol which
must be used to upload the file.
The FTP URL is of format: ftp://User:password@host:port/path in which the parts User:
password@, :password or :port may be excluded.
The Charging Station has a required Configuration Variable that reports which file transfer
protocols it supports: FileTransferProtocols
The format of the log file is not prescribed.
FTP needs to be able to use Passive FTP, to be able to transverse over as much different
typologies as possible.

# N01 - Requirements

**N01.FR.01**
Precondition: Upon receipt of a GetLogRequest AND
if the requested log information is
available
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a
GetLogResponse stating the name of the file and
status Accepted.

**N01.FR.02**
Precondition: N01.FR.01
The Charging Station SHALL start uploading a
single log file to the specified location

**N01.FR.03**
Precondition: N01.FR.02
AND
The GetLogRequest contained
logType SecurityLog
The Charging Station SHALL upload its security log

**N01.FR.04**
Precondition: N01.FR.02
AND
The GetLogRequest contained
logType DiagnosticsLog
The Charging Station SHALL upload its diagnostics.

**N01.FR.05**
Precondition: Upon receipt of a GetLogRequest AND
if the requested log information is
NOT available
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a
GetLogResponse WITH status Rejected.

**N01.FR.07**
Every LogStatusNotificationRequest sent for a log
upload SHALL contain the same requestId as the
GetLogRequest that started this log upload.

**N01.FR.08**
Precondition: When uploading a log document is
started
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status
Uploading.

**N01.FR.09**
Precondition: When a log document is uploaded
successfully
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status
Uploaded.

**N01.FR.10**
Precondition: When uploading a log document failed
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status
UploadFailure, BadMessage,
PermissionDenied OR
NotSupportedOperation.

**N01.FR.12**
Precondition: When a Charging Station is
assembling or uploading the log file
AND
the Charging Station receives a new
GetLogRequest
The Charging Station SHOULD cancel the ongoing
log file upload AND respond with GetLogResponse
with status AcceptedCanceled.

**N01.FR.13**
The field requestId in LogStatusNotificationRequest
is mandatory, unless the message was triggered by
a TriggerMessageRequest AND there is no log
upload ongoing.

**N01.FR.14**
It is RECOMMENDED that Charging Station and
CSMS support at least HTTP(s) as transport
mechanism for the log file upload

**N01.FR.15**
Charging Station SHALL at least support the CSMS
trust chain for secure transports

**N01.FR.16**
It is RECOMMENDED that Charging Station
supports the usual CAs provided by the operating
system

# N01 - Requirements (continued)

**N01.FR.17**
Precondition: When CSMS requires basic
authorization for the upload
CSMS is RECOMMENDED to require a different
basic authorization password for the upload, then
the one used for OCPP connectivity.

**N01.FR.18**
Is is RECOMMENDED that CSMS accepts both PUT
and POST requests for uploads from Charging
Station.

**N01.FR.19**
Precondition: When Charging Station uses a
HTTP(s) POST request to upload the
log file
Charging Station SHALL provide at least the
following attributes: Content-Type: (e.g.
application/octet-stream) and Content-
Disposition: with a specification of the
filename.

**N01.FR.20**
Precondition: N01.FR.12 AND
Charging Station cancels the log file
upload
The Charging Station SHALL send a
LogStatusNotificationRequest with status =
AcceptedCanceled.

**N01.FR.21**
Precondition: N01.FR.18 AND
the GetLogRequest
log.remoteLocation string ends with a
slash character ("/")
The Charging Station SHALL append the filename
that it returned in the GetLogResponse filename
field to this string.

**N01.FR.22**
Precondition: N01.FR.18 AND
the file to upload exceeds the
threshold of 1MB (1024 * 1024 bytes)
The Charging Station MAY include the "Expect: 100-
continue" header in the HTTP PUT request.

**N01.FR.23**
Precondition: N01.FR.18
The Charging Station MAY implement a mechanism
to resume HTTP uploads, if it can identify whether
the upload server supports the same mechanism.
